arts.ttl: Report: Bayern Munich has agreed to personal terms with Chelsea’s Christopher Nkunku
arts.sum: Somehow, though, that is only the second craziest thing happening at the moment — Bayern Munich could be on the verge of an acquisition of Chelsea FC attacker Christopher Nkunku, who made a name for himself in the Bundesliga at RB Leipzig. Per Sky Sport journalist Florian Plettenberg, Bayern Munich has already agreed to personal terms with Nkunku and is making him a priority target for this month (as captured by @iMiaSanMia ):

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich vs. Hoffenheim: 2024/25 Bundesliga full coverage
arts.sum: Bayern Munich and Hoffenheim are set for a midweek collision in a match-up that appears to be lopsided if you look at the Bundesliga table. However, the visitors have tended to give Bayern Munich a run for their money in recent years, so this should be a very intriguing match-up to say the least.

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Blues 'make contact' with Bayern Munich over potential move for attacker
arts.sum: Chelsea reportedly make contact with German giants Bayern Munich over the situation of 19-year-old forward Mathys Tel.

arts.ttl: Transfer news: Chelsea to trade Christopher Nkunku for Bayern Munich talent Mathys Tel?

arts.ttl: Chelsea's Christopher Nkunku 'pushing to join' Bayern Munich as Stamford Bridge departure gathers pace
arts.sum: Chelsea forward Christopher Nkunku is reportedly pushing for a move to German giants Bayern Munich after struggling to establish himself as a first-choice option at Stamford Bridge.

arts.ttl: Update: As Chelsea chats about Mathys Tel, Christopher Nkunku reportedly wants move to Bayern Munich
arts.sum: According to a report from Fabrizio Romano, Chelsea FC has held talks with Bayern Munich in recent days over...Mathys Tel. Things might be a little spicy between Bayern Munich and Chelsea FC as the two clubs talk about a deal for Mathys Tel. arts.ttl: Chelsea make enquiry for teenage Bayern winger Tel
arts.sum: Bayern Munich's 19-year-old winger Mathys Tel is a target for the Blues during the January transfer window with the German side interested in Chelsea forward Christopher Nkunku.
